findings.message: New mutating route `archive_item` (POST /items/{item_id}/archive) has no domain-level instrumentation: no structured log of the archive action, no metric/counter for archive attempts vs. successes/failures, and no custom trace span/attributes. Request-level middleware captures latency/status counters but the business event (archiving an item) is invisible to logs, metrics, and traces — a new untraced/unmetered code path.
findings.suggestion: Inject a logger (e.g. `from demo.logging_config import get_logger`) and emit a structured log on success (`log.info('item_archived', item_id=item_id)`); add a counter for archive operations to MetricsRegistry (or recoverability) and/or annotate the current span with `item_id` and outcome via OpenTelemetry.

findings.message: The 404 error path (`item is None` -> HTTPException) is raised without any structured log carrying the correlation/request id. Operators cannot correlate 'item not found' failures back to a specific request, and the event never reaches logs at all (HTTPException is handled by FastAPI's default handler, not the recoverability path).
findings.suggestion: Log the not-found path with the correlation id before raising, e.g. `log.warning('archive_item_not_found', item_id=item_id)` (the logging config / middleware should bind the correlation id automatically), so 404s on this route are observable.

findings.message: State mutation (`session.commit()` on an item rename) is performed with no audit/info log and no metric. For a mutating endpoint this leaves no trail of what was archived and when — a context-propagation/observability gap for write operations.
findings.suggestion: Emit an info log after the successful commit including item_id and (optionally) the prior name, and bump a domain metric (e.g. `app_items_archived_total`) so archive throughput is visible on the dashboard.

findings.message: `HTTPException` is imported inside the function body, which prevents linters/import-time hooks (and any future tracing instrumentation that wraps imports) from seeing the dependency and slightly fragments the error-handling story for this route.
findings.suggestion: Move `from fastapi import HTTPException` to the module-level import block alongside `APIRouter`/`Depends`.
